name: As Nogais - circular route along the Navia River - Cruzul-Agüeira Ecological Conservation Area
name_original: As Nogais — circular por el río Navia — Zona Ecológica de Conservación Cruzul-Agüeira
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around As Nogais are situated in the mountainous Os Ancares region of eastern Lugo, Galicia, featuring a diverse and rugged landscape. The terrain is defined by narrow, deeply incised river valleys and elevated mountain ranges, with peaks like Rañadoiro and Piornal reaching over 1300 meters. This varied topography provides a dynamic environment for mountain biking, offering both challenging ascents and exhilarating descents. The area is also part of the Biosphere Reserve of Os Ancares, preserving its natural beauty with Atlantic and Mediterranean forests. Best mountain bike trails around As Nogais The most popular mountain bike trail is As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views across the conservation area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail, a difficult 21.9 miles (35.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and panoramic vistas from Pico dos Tres Bispos. Local mountain bikers also love the Hiking from Teso da Lama Figueiro y Becerreá to As Nogais - circular route through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail leading through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around As Nogais is defined by narrow river valleys, elevated mountain ranges, and Atlantic and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in As Nogais are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore As Nogais's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around As Nogais?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails available around As Nogais, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. The region's diverse terrain, with its narrow river valleys and elevated mountain ranges, provides a dynamic environment for cycling.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in As Nogais?
A: Mountain bike trails in As Nogais feature a diverse and rugged landscape, characterized by deeply incised river valleys and mountain ranges reaching over 1300 meters. You can expect a mix of challenging ascents, exhilarating descents, technical sections, and singletrack paths, often traversing Atlantic and Mediterranean forests within the Biosphere Reserve of Os Ancares.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in As Nogais?
A: Yes, there are moderate mountain bike trails available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211375416/as-nogais-circular-route-along-the-navia-river-cruzul-agueeira-ecological-conservation-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais - circular route along the Navia River - Cruzul-Agüeira Ecological Conservation Area&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.4 km route that takes approximately 1 hour 20 minutes to complete, offering a less strenuous option while still enjoying the natural beauty.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in As Nogais?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210569136/mosteiro-de-santa-maria-de-penamaior-from-becerrea-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosteiro de Santa María de Penamaior from Becerreá — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29.4 km route with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211428925/hiking-from-teso-da-lama-figueiro-y-becerrea-to-as-nogais-circular-route-through-the-special-conservation-area-cruzul-agueeira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking from Teso da Lama Figueiro y Becerreá to As Nogais - circular route through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.7 km trail through the Cruzul-Agüeira Special Conservation Area.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in As Nogais?
A: Yes, many routes in As Nogais are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211455428/as-nogais-desde-piedrafita-circular-por-la-zona-especial-de-conservacion-los-ancares-courel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210510282/pico-dos-tres-bispos-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, both offering extensive views and challenging terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with scenic viewpoints in As Nogais?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210510282/pico-dos-tres-bispos-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers magnificent vistas from Pico dos Tres Bispos. Additionally, high viewpoints like As Nogais itself provide sweeping views of the Navia River valley and the historic Torre de Doncos.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around As Nogais offer access to several notable attractions. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut Tree in Ramil&lt;/a&gt;, an 800-year-old natural monument, or the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Statue at Alto de San Roque&lt;/a&gt;, which offers unique panoramic views at 1270 meters. The Camino de Santiago also traverses the region, blending cultural and historical elements with the natural landscape.

Q: Is the Camino de Santiago suitable for mountain biking in this area?
A: Yes, sections of the renowned Camino de Santiago pilgrimage route traverse the region around As Nogais. These established paths are suitable for cycling, allowing mountain bikers to combine the adventure of trail riding with cultural exploration and historical significance.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in As Nogais?
A: The mountain bike trails around As Nogais are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars. Riders often praise the diverse and rugged landscape, the challenging ascents and exhilarating descents, and the stunning panoramic views from elevated mountain ranges and viewpoints.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in As Nogais?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the mountainous region of Os Ancares generally offers good conditions for outdoor activities during spring, summer, and early autumn. These periods typically provide more stable weather and accessible trails, allowing riders to fully enjoy the diverse terrain and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, many trails in As Nogais are situated within or pass through protected areas. The region is part of the Biosphere Reserve of Os Ancares, highlighting its significant ecological value. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211455428/as-nogais-desde-piedrafita-circular-por-la-zona-especial-de-conservacion-los-ancares-courel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive views across this conservation area.
tips: Nestled in the landscape of the Navia river valley, the medieval defensive watchtower, the Tower of Doncos is a unique historical complex.
